original text:
Use of Pad Mounted transformers in Group Housing Societies.
Release of connection to D.S.& NDS consumers on three phase supply.
Captive power plant policy and issues connected with installation of CPP/IPPS & Co-Generation Plants.
Restoration of supply to the defaulting premises without recovery of outstanding dues.
Construction of residences/ other buildings beneath LT/HT lines.
Metering arrangement for plastic and Rubber industries.
Independent feeder for Government Hospitals, Public Health Water Works, Defence Services, Telephone Exchange, All India Radio, Railway and Educational Institutions having total connected load of 100 KW or more  addition to SMI No. 2.7.
TDCO facility to Rice Millers, Cotton Industries and food processing industries.
Appeal to the Power Consumers regarding meter and service cable.
Release of Power connection to the tubewells under AP categoryRevival of condition regarding hold of minimum Agriculture land for getting out of turn priority in respect of applicants belonging to SC/disabled/Handi capped categories.
Designation of Appellate Authority as provided under Section-127 of the Electricity Act-2003.
Levy of fuel surcharge adjustment (FSA).
Ban on use of phase changer in case of three phase supply consumers.
No Demand Certificate to be issued by DHBVN regarding no dues with regard to payment of electricity pending to the consumers (Agriculturist) for grant of exemption from payment of stamp duty on mortgage deed.
Metering on Agriculture Power Tubewell connections.
Detailed investigation of rural domestic consumption for unusual high billingceiling thereof.
Terminology of Electronics meters as Digital meters.
Re-utilization of electronic meters dismantled from consumer premises.
Guidelines for providing independent feeder to private Hospitals/ Nursing Homes Commercial Consumers like shopping Malls and shopping Centres.
HERC Regulations No.HERC/12/2005 on Duty to Supply on Request under section-43 of the Electricity Act-2003.
Instructions for dealing with the cases of theft of electricity.
Levy of surcharge on power consumers in DHBVN viz-a-viz with holding of implementation thereof.
HERC regulation No.12/HERC/12/2005 on Power to require Security under Sec.47 of the Electricity Act-2003interest thereupon.
Pendency of application for release of new Industrial connection in Gurgaon /Faridabad.
Release of connections to Domestic and Non-domestic consumers providing of meters.
Grant of more than one connection in the same premises.
Implementation of short term open access within the State of Haryana and Transmission and other related charges to be recovered from the short term Open Access Customers.
Installation of meter out side the consumer premises after detection of theft of electricity /unauthorized use of electricity .
Guidelines/Modalities for release of electric connections in Police Line Colonies/other Govt. Colonies.
Payment of Municipal tax & service charges etc. in Electricity Bills to local Bodies in respect of Central Govt. offices.
Providing Check meters at the poles in case of LT consumers.
Settlement of the Pending Electricity Bills of Rural Domestic and Agriculture categories of consumer in rural areas - Clarification.
Shifting/replacement of single phase and three phase meters on selected 11 KV feeders in DHBVN.
Release of tubewell connections-identification of site through G.P.S. ( Global Positioning Satellites ).
Installation of meters outside the consumers premises while releasing new connections or replacement of meter.
Pendency of applications for release of new Industrial connection in Gurgaon/Faridabad .
HERC regulation on duty to supply electricity on request - self execution of works clarification.
Seeking of temporary disconnection facility by Industrial consumers.
Release of new connection in un-approved colonies.
Amendment in SMI 1.11 relating to Competency of officers for sanction/extension of load.
Penalty for unauthorized extension of load of LT consumers resulting into change of category from LT to HT industrial-clarification thereon.
Shifting/replacement of single phase and three phase meters on selected 11 KV feeders in DHBVN-Clarification.
Levy of fuel surcharge adjustment (FSA) claim.
Billing cycle of DS and NDS category.

original text:
Honolulu, Hawaii, Oct. 30, 2018 - Hawaii-based agriculture technology company Smart Yields has developed a new program to help small- and medium-size farmers form collaborative partnerships with research, educational, industry and community partners throughout the U.S. The #AlohaAg Challenge is designed around the needs of horticulture regions affected by climate and weather issues such as late frost.
The program focuses on workforce development through the deployment of technology and shared data. Smart Yields supplies state-of-the-art sensors to farmers to monitor everything from soil and water inputs to weather and pests, while partner agencies tap into a national network of data. This collaborative approach allows farmers to gain access to leading crop technologies for as little as 50 cents a day, while partners support agricultural best practices and the growth of agriculture jobs in local and regional communities.
Through shared data, the program is also designed to create and accelerate regional solutions to pressing challenges in agriculture, like climate change. Each community needs a stakeholder sponsor and a minimum of 42 farmers to sign up.
Farmers and partners interested in learning more are encouraged to visit smartyields.com/solutions or contact Smart Yields at [email protected] for additional information.
Smart Yields connects farmers, agricultural researchers and their communities through crowd-sourced data gathered from a long-range network of integrated, state-of-the-art sensors that measure everything from soil health to inputs such as water, energy and nutrients. The company recently launched an extensive pilot program with the Rocky Mountain Farmers Union in Colorado, covering nearly 1 million acres and supplying dozens of fruit growers with data that has saved hundreds of thousands of dollars in high-value crop losses. The company's global expansion includes markets in Europe and Asia, as well as the establishment of Centers of Excellence to encourage farming best practices.
